sglang.title: Fix flashinfer version
sglang.author: PanJason
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
The interface of flashinfer has changed from version 0.0.6 to 0.0.7. Instead of taking the bool value logits_cap, it now takes a float logits_soft_cap, which replaces the 30 in the original calculation. See issue #575
One thing I am not sure now is whether I need to add support for version 0.0.5 and

vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
ActorDiedError is not present in older versions of Ray, so we use RayActorError instead.
Closes #6033
Closes #5861

vllm.title: [Bugfix] adding chunking mechanism to fused_moe to handle large inputs
vllm.author: avshalomman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This fix provides a hotfix for issue 5938 where the fused_moe kernel crashes on long inputs (> 64k tokens).
Instead of asserting as suggested in this commit, we execute the kernel in chunks and aggregate the outputs. This enables us to process very large inputs (batched or single prompt) without cra

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Add explicit `end_forward` calls to flashinfer
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Without and explicit end_forward call, the intermediate buffers may have leftover garbage data, affecting results.

vllm.title: [BugFix] Ensure worker model loop is always stopped at the right time
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The model loop in the worker processes is meant to stop when there are no more sequences to process but the condition being checked for this wasn't sufficient, meaning that the workers can incorrectly remain in an indefinite broadcast loop.
Resolves #5987

vllm.title: [Model] Changes to MLPSpeculator to support tie_weights and input_scale
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
New versions of the MLPSpeculator models have two new features:
tie_weights
input_scale

In particular, the former allows us to significantly reduce the size of the speculator model.
This PR adds support in vLLM for these new features.

vllm.title: [Core] Optimize block_manager_v2 vs block_manager_v1 (to make V2 default)
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR optimizes block_manager_v2 python logic to make it comparable to block_manager_v1. The goal is to enable block_manager_v2 by default as part of the spec decode project.
The issues optimized are:

Python Block object allocations/deallocations are expensive on the hot-path of iterative batchin

vllm.title: [Frontend] Add template related params to request
vllm.author: danieljannai21
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
HuggingFace's apply_chat_template functions allows sending additional parameters to either change the chat template or its behavior. I added chat_template and template_kwargs to the ChatCompletionRequest class to allow full control over the behavior of apply_chat_template.
